title: Intensely Nutty Tunisian Olive Tagine
description: A kid-friendly Tunisian dish featuring olives, kombu-cold-extract, and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients, resulting in a nutty and gooey flavor profile. This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the sliced almonds and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they are lightly toasted and fragrant.
Add the pitted olives, kombu cold extract, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan.
Stir all the ingredients together and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the tagine cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the intensely nutty Tunisian olive tagine hot, garnished with additional sliced almonds and a sprinkle of cumin if desired.
